fields.Scope: Large organisations meeting 2 of 3 criteria: 250+ employees, GBP 36M+ turnover, GBP 18M+ total assets. Also covers all companies regardless of size for some provisions.
fields.Enforcement: Corporate criminal liability. Unlimited fines. SFO and CPS prosecution. Reasonable procedures defence available.
fields.Relevance Notes: The UK Economic Crime Act creates corporate criminal liability for failure to prevent fraud, with unlimited fines for large organizations. Companies need intelligence on fraud typologies, criminal networks, and emerging threats to demonstrate reasonable prevention procedures. Revontulet provides financial crime intelligence and fraud pattern analysis that supports clients' prevention frameworks.

fields.Full Name: Regulation (EU) 2024/1183 amending Regulation (EU) No 910/2014 establishing the European Digital Identity Framework
fields.Brief Description: Mandates EU Digital Identity Wallets for all Member States by end of 2026. Citizens can store and share verified credentials, enabling privacy-preserving attribute exchange. Introduces Qualified Electronic Attestation of Attributes for trusted digital identity verification.
fields.Next Steps: Member States must issue at least one EU Digital Identity Wallet by December 2026. Mandatory acceptance by all public and semi-public organisations by the same date.
fields.Scope: All EU Member States, public sector organisations, and private entities relying on electronic identification. Mandatory wallet issuance and acceptance framework.
fields.Enforcement: National supervisory bodies for trust services. Implementation monitored through EU-wide interoperability requirements.
fields.Relevance Notes: The EU Digital Identity Wallet creates new vectors for identity fraud and social engineering as citizens store credentials digitally. Organizations implementing eIDAS 2.0 need intelligence on identity fraud techniques, deepfake risks, and social engineering tactics. Revontulet provides threat intelligence on digital identity exploitation methods that helps clients protect their eIDAS implementations.

fields.Full Name: UN Security Council Resolution 2178 (2014) - Foreign Terrorist Fighters
fields.Brief Description: Binding UN resolution on foreign terrorist fighters. Requires states to prevent recruitment, develop watch lists, share cross-border intelligence, and enhance border security. Chapter VII enforcement.
fields.Next Steps: Strengthened by UNSCR 2396 (2017) which added biometric data, PNR, and API requirements. Ongoing CTC/CTED monitoring of national implementation.
fields.Scope: All UN member states. Requires prevention and suppression of recruitment, organization, transport, and equipping of foreign terrorist fighters.
fields.Enforcement: Chapter VII binding. CTC/CTED assessment visits. Requires states to develop watch lists, share information, and enhance border security.
fields.Relevance Notes: UNSCR 2178 requires states to prevent recruitment and travel of foreign terrorist fighters, necessitating cross-domain intelligence connecting terrorism, travel patterns, financial flows, and online radicalization. Revontulet provides the integrated counter-terrorism intelligence that governments and transport sector entities need to implement effective FTF prevention measures.

fields.Full Name: Children's Online Privacy Protection Act
fields.Brief Description: US federal law requiring parental consent for collecting personal data from children under 13, with strict rules on data use, storage, and deletion by online services.
fields.Relevance Notes: COPPA requires parental consent for collecting data from children under 13, with significant penalties for non-compliance. Platforms and online service providers need robust age verification and data handling processes. Revontulet provides intelligence on exploitation patterns and platform safety risks that helps clients understand the threat landscape underpinning child safety regulations.

fields.Full Name: National Security and Investment Act 2021
fields.Brief Description: Mandatory foreign investment screening across 17 sensitive sectors including defence, energy, AI, and communications. Transactions can be blocked or unwound. Penalties up to 5% of worldwide turnover.
fields.Next Steps: Fully operational since Jan 2022. 1,000+ notifications reviewed. Investment Security Unit publishes annual reports. Mandatory notification for 17 sensitive sectors.
fields.Scope: Acquisitions of entities or assets in 17 sensitive sectors including defence, energy, transport, AI, communications, and computing hardware. Mandatory notification regime.
fields.Enforcement: Investment Security Unit (Cabinet Office). Transactions can be blocked, unwound, or subject to conditions. Civil penalties up to 5% of worldwide turnover. Criminal penalties up to 5 years imprisonment.
fields.Relevance Notes: The UK's mandatory FDI screening covers 17 sensitive sectors including defence, AI, and cybersecurity. Investors and companies undergoing screening need intelligence on foreign actors, state affiliations, and investor networks. Revontulet provides geopolitical risk assessments and actor network analysis that supports clients navigating UK investment screening processes.

fields.Full Name: Information Technology (Intermediary Guidelines and Digital Media Ethics Code) Rules, 2021 (as amended 2023)
fields.Brief Description: India's intermediary guidelines requiring proactive content monitoring and message traceability for platforms with 5M+ users. Non-compliance triggers loss of safe harbour protection.
fields.Next Steps: In force. 2023 amendments added AI/deep fake provisions and government fact-check unit. Traceability of first originator requirement for messaging platforms with 5M+ users. 36-hour content removal for court/government orders.
fields.Scope: All social media intermediaries in India. Significant Social Media Intermediaries (SSMIs) with 5M+ registered users face enhanced obligations including traceability and proactive monitoring.
fields.Enforcement: MeitY enforcement. Loss of safe harbour protection for non-compliant intermediaries. Criminal liability under IT Act Section 69.
fields.Relevance Notes: India's IT Rules require proactive content monitoring and message traceability for first originators of terrorist and criminal content. Platforms operating in India need intelligence on extremist content networks and organized crime communication patterns. Revontulet provides the content classification and network analysis capabilities that support compliance with India's intermediary guidelines.

fields.Full Name: ISO/IEC 27032:2023 - Cybersecurity - Guidelines for Internet security
fields.Brief Description: International cybersecurity guidelines covering Internet security, multi-stakeholder coordination, and threat information sharing. Provides the framework for cross-sector cooperation on cyber threats.
fields.Next Steps: Revised 2023. Covers Internet security guidelines including multi-stakeholder coordination, threat landscape analysis, and information sharing frameworks.
fields.Scope: Organizations addressing Internet security risks. Covers guidance on information sharing, coordination, and incident handling for cyberspace stakeholders.
fields.Enforcement: Voluntary guideline standard. Provides cybersecurity framework for coordination between stakeholders.
fields.Relevance Notes: ISO 27032 provides the international framework for multi-stakeholder cybersecurity coordination and information sharing. Organizations implementing coordinated cyber defense need reliable intelligence partners. Revontulet's threat intelligence services align with ISO 27032's information sharing frameworks, enabling clients to participate effectively in collaborative cybersecurity ecosystems.

fields.Full Name: Foreign Influence Transparency Scheme Act 2018 (strengthened 2024)
fields.Brief Description: Requires registration of activities undertaken on behalf of foreign governments or political organizations. Criminal penalties up to 7 years for non-registration. Strengthened in 2024.
fields.Next Steps: Operational since 2019. Strengthened through Foreign Interference through Social Media Bill 2024. Ongoing expansion of transparency obligations for foreign-influenced activities.
fields.Scope: Any person or entity undertaking activities in Australia on behalf of a foreign principal (foreign government, government-related entity, foreign political organisation).
fields.Enforcement: Criminal penalties for non-registration. Up to 7 years imprisonment for aggravated offences. Civil penalty provisions.
fields.Relevance Notes: Australia's Foreign Influence Transparency Scheme requires registration of activities undertaken on behalf of foreign governments. Revontulet provides intelligence on state-sponsored influence networks, foreign interference operations, and covert political activities that helps clients identify and assess foreign influence risks in the Australian context.

fields.Full Name: 개인정보보호법 (Personal Information Protection Act, amended 2023)
fields.Brief Description: South Korea's personal data protection law with 2023 amendments strengthening cross-border transfer rules. Fines up to 3% of revenue with criminal penalties for serious violations.
fields.Next Steps: Amended 2023 with expanded enforcement. 72-hour breach notification. Personal Information Protection Commission (PIPC) active enforcement.
fields.Scope: All entities processing personal information of Korean residents. Cross-border transfer provisions apply to international companies.
fields.Enforcement: PIPC enforcement. Fines up to 3% of revenue. Criminal penalties for serious violations.
fields.Relevance Notes: South Korea's strengthened data protection law with cross-border transfer requirements affects organizations operating in one of Asia's largest technology markets. Revontulet provides cyber threat intelligence and data protection risk context relevant to the Korean regulatory environment, supporting clients' PIPA compliance efforts.

fields.Full Name: National Security Act 2023 (c. 32)
fields.Brief Description: The most significant reform of UK espionage law in a century. Creates new offences for espionage, sabotage, and foreign interference. Introduces the Foreign Influence Registration Scheme (FIRS). Life imprisonment for espionage and sabotage offences.
fields.Next Steps: Core measures in force since December 2023. FIRS provisions being implemented in phases. Already used in high-profile prosecutions including alleged Chinese intelligence operations.
fields.Scope: Any person conducting activities in the UK on behalf of or for the benefit of a foreign power. FIRS requires registration of political influence activities and certain other activities directed by foreign states posing enhanced risk.
fields.Enforcement: UK security services, National Crime Agency, and CPS. Life imprisonment for espionage and sabotage. Foreign interference offence carries up to 14 years. FIRS non-compliance is a criminal offence.
fields.Relevance Notes: The UK's most significant espionage law reform in a century creates new offences and a Foreign Influence Registration Scheme. Organizations need intelligence on state-sponsored espionage, sabotage threats, and foreign interference activities. Revontulet provides the threat analysis on foreign state operations and influence networks that helps clients assess and mitigate national security risks.

fields.Full Name: Privacy Protection Law Amendment 13 (תיקון 13 לחוק הגנת הפרטיות)
fields.Brief Description: Modernizes Israel's data protection regime with GDPR-style obligations. Fines up to 5% of annual turnover. INCD exercises separate regulatory authority over critical infrastructure cybersecurity.
fields.Next Steps: In force since Aug 2025. Major overhaul aligning with GDPR. Israel National Cyber Directorate (INCD) operates as national threat intelligence hub.
fields.Scope: All entities processing personal data of Israeli residents. INCD covers all critical infrastructure and government entities.
fields.Enforcement: Privacy Protection Authority enforcement. Fines up to 5% of annual turnover. INCD has separate regulatory authority over critical infrastructure.
fields.Relevance Notes: Israel's modernized data protection regime with GDPR-style obligations affects organizations in one of the world's most advanced cyber ecosystems. Revontulet provides intelligence on the unique threat landscape facing Israeli entities, combining cyber threat analysis with geopolitical context relevant to the region's security environment.

fields.Full Name: Cyber Security Act 2024 (complementing Security of Critical Infrastructure Act 2018)
fields.Brief Description: Introduces mandatory ransomware payment reporting, minimum security standards for smart devices, and a Cyber Incident Review Board. Complements the SOCI Act's critical infrastructure protections.
fields.Next Steps: Ransomware payment reporting obligations effective since May 2025. Security standards for smart devices to be set through regulations.
fields.Scope: Critical infrastructure operators under SOCI Act, organizations above AUD 3M annual revenue, entities making ransomware payments.
fields.Enforcement: Civil penalties for failure to report ransomware payments. SOCI Act enforcement for critical infrastructure operators. Limited use provisions to encourage voluntary reporting.
fields.Relevance Notes: Australia's Cyber Security Act introduces mandatory ransomware payment reporting, minimum device security standards, and limited-use protections for incident data. Revontulet provides intelligence on ransomware groups, criminal organizations, and state-sponsored threats that helps Australian entities meet their reporting obligations and implement effective security measures.

fields.Full Name: UN Security Council Resolution 2462 (2019) - Countering the Financing of Terrorism
fields.Brief Description: Binding UN resolution strengthening counter-terrorism financing frameworks. Requires robust CFT risk assessments and private sector cooperation. Complements and reinforces FATF standards globally.
fields.Next Steps: Ongoing. Strengthens UNSCR 1373 with specific focus on terrorist financing through new payment methods, social media fundraising, and abuse of NPOs.
fields.Scope: All UN member states. Requires robust CFT frameworks, risk assessments, and cooperation with private sector and financial institutions.
fields.Enforcement: Chapter VII binding. Complements FATF standards. CTC monitoring.
fields.Relevance Notes: UNSCR 2462 strengthens counter-terrorism financing frameworks with specific attention to emerging methods including cryptocurrency, social media fundraising, and crowdfunding. Revontulet provides intelligence on these evolving terrorist financing typologies, helping clients detect and report suspicious financing activity through non-traditional channels.

fields.Full Name: Bill C-8, An Act respecting cyber security (Critical Cyber Systems Protection Act)
fields.Brief Description: Canada's critical cyber systems protection bill covering federally regulated telecom, finance, energy, and transport operators. Establishes mandatory cybersecurity programs and incident reporting.
fields.Next Steps: Second reading completed Oct 2025. Under study by SECU committee. Original Bill C-26 died on the Order Paper during Jan 2025 prorogation. Expected to pass with mandatory incident reporting.
fields.Scope: Operators of critical cyber systems in federally regulated sectors: telecommunications, finance, energy, and transport.
fields.Enforcement: Penalties for non-compliance with cybersecurity directions. Specific framework to be finalized through committee process.
fields.Relevance Notes: Canada's critical cyber systems protection bill covers federally regulated sectors including telecom, finance, energy, and transport. Revontulet provides the cross-domain threat intelligence that Canadian critical infrastructure operators need to meet their cyber protection obligations, with relevant experience from analogous European frameworks like NIS2.

fields.Full Name: Wassenaar Arrangement on Export Controls for Conventional Arms and Dual-Use Goods and Technologies
fields.Brief Description: Multilateral export control regime with 43 participating states governing international transfers of conventional arms and dual-use goods. Controls on intrusion software, IP-network surveillance, and cyber-surveillance tools updated at 2024 plenary. Underpins EU Dual-Use Regulation.
fields.Next Steps: Active. Control lists updated annually by consensus at plenary sessions. 2024 plenary adopted updated controls on intrusion software and IP-network surveillance systems.
fields.Scope: 43 participating states coordinate export controls on conventional arms and dual-use goods and technologies. Five categories of cyber-surveillance tools on the control list since 2013.
fields.Enforcement: Voluntary regime operating by consensus. Participating states implement through national export control legislation. No central enforcement mechanism.
fields.Relevance Notes: The Wassenaar Arrangement's 43 participating states control exports of cyber-surveillance and intelligence technologies. Organizations developing or deploying such tools need to understand export restrictions and end-use controls. Revontulet provides guidance on the regulatory implications of intelligence technology transfers and geopolitical risk assessments for export decisions.

fields.Full Name: Kids Online Safety Act
fields.Brief Description: US federal law requiring platforms to provide minors with safeguards including opt-out of addictive features, strongest privacy defaults, and tools to limit harmful content exposure.
fields.Relevance Notes: KOSA requires platforms to provide minors with safeguards against addictive design features and harmful content, with new duty-of-care obligations. Platforms subject to KOSA need intelligence on exploitation tactics, harmful content patterns, and platform safety risks. Revontulet provides threat analysis that supports clients' broader online safety compliance efforts.

fields.Full Name: Bundesgesetz über die Bekämpfung der Geldwäscherei (Geldwäschereigesetz, GwG) - 2025 Revision
fields.Brief Description: Modernizes Swiss AML law to cover lawyers, notaries, and trust advisors. Introduces a federal beneficial ownership registry. Addresses FATF mutual evaluation gaps. FINMA enforcement.
fields.Next Steps: Comprehensive revision adopted Sept 2025. Entry into force expected mid-2026. Expands scope to lawyers/notaries as 'advisors.' Establishes federal Transparency Register (LETA) for beneficial ownership.
fields.Scope: Financial intermediaries, banks, VASPs, and newly: lawyers, notaries, and trust service providers acting as advisors. Federal beneficial ownership registry.
fields.Enforcement: FINMA enforcement for financial sector. Federal Office of Justice for registry. Criminal penalties for AML violations.
fields.Relevance Notes: Switzerland's modernized AML framework extends to lawyers, notaries, and trust advisors, with a new beneficial ownership register. Switzerland's concentrated financial sector makes its AML framework globally significant. Revontulet provides the financial crime intelligence, beneficial ownership analysis, and risk assessments that Swiss financial intermediaries need for enhanced GwG compliance.

fields.Full Name: Regulation (EU) 2023/1114 on Markets in Crypto-Assets
fields.Brief Description: The EU's first comprehensive framework for crypto-asset regulation. Requires licensing for crypto-asset service providers (CASPs), prohibits insider trading and market manipulation in crypto markets, and mandates Travel Rule compliance for originator/beneficiary data on transfers.
fields.Next Steps: Fully applicable since Dec 2024 for CASPs. Transitional period with EU-wide deadline of July 2026. ESMA and national regulators overseeing implementation.
fields.Scope: Crypto-asset service providers, issuers of asset-referenced tokens and e-money tokens operating within the EU. Covers exchanges, custodial wallets, and advisory services.
fields.Enforcement: ESMA and national competent authorities. Administrative sanctions and measures including withdrawal of authorisation. Market abuse provisions mirror traditional financial regulation.
fields.Relevance Notes: MiCA creates the EU's first comprehensive crypto-asset regulation, requiring licensing and AML compliance for crypto service providers. Crypto-assets are increasingly exploited for terrorist financing, money laundering, and sanctions evasion. Revontulet provides blockchain intelligence, financial crime analysis, and sanctions screening capabilities that help crypto-asset service providers meet MiCA's compliance requirements.

fields.Full Name: ISO/IEC 25389:2025 - Cybersecurity Maturity Model and Metrics
fields.Brief Description: International standard establishing cybersecurity maturity models and metrics for organizations to assess and improve their security posture.
fields.Relevance Notes: ISO 25389 establishes cybersecurity maturity models and metrics for measuring organizational security posture. Revontulet provides threat intelligence and risk assessment capabilities that inform maturity assessments, helping clients identify gaps and prioritize security investments aligned with international benchmarking standards.

fields.Full Name: Norwegian Security Act (Lov om nasjonal sikkerhet)
fields.Brief Description: Norway's national security framework mandates risk-based protective security across critical infrastructure, with the new Digital Security Act introducing cybersecurity obligations and fines up to 4% of turnover.
fields.Next Steps: Digital Security Act (digitalsikkerhetsloven) transposing NIS2 entered into force 1 Jan 2025. Implementing regulations ongoing.
fields.Scope: Entities of significance for national security designated by sector ministries. Covers critical infrastructure, government, defence supply chain.
fields.Enforcement: NSM oversight. Criminal penalties for breaches. Administrative sanctions.
fields.Relevance Notes: Norway's national security framework mandates risk-based protective security for critical infrastructure and national security interests. Organizations subject to Sikkerhetsloven need comprehensive threat assessments covering espionage, sabotage, and terrorism. Revontulet provides the multi-domain threat intelligence and security risk analysis that supports clients' protective security obligations under Norwegian law.

fields.Full Name: Foreign Agents Registration Act (22 U.S.C. 611 et seq.)
fields.Brief Description: Requires agents of foreign principals to register with the DOJ and disclose their activities, including political propaganda and lobbying. Major proposed regulatory modernisation published January 2025, first significant update in nearly 30 years.
fields.Next Steps: In force. DOJ published proposed rulemaking January 2025 modernising registration requirements, narrowing commercial exemptions, and updating digital labelling requirements for informational materials.
fields.Scope: Any person or entity acting in the US as an agent of a foreign principal, including foreign governments, political parties, and persons outside the US. Covers political, public relations, and lobbying activities.
fields.Enforcement: DOJ National Security Division, FARA Unit. Criminal penalties including up to 5 years imprisonment and $250K fines. Active enforcement with multiple high-profile prosecutions in 2024.
fields.Relevance Notes: FARA registration data and enforcement actions are valuable open-source intelligence on foreign influence operations in the United States. Revontulet leverages FARA disclosures and enforcement patterns to provide clients with intelligence on foreign influence networks, lobbying activities, and state-sponsored advocacy that informs their geopolitical risk assessments.

fields.Full Name: Online Safety Act 2021 (Cth)
fields.Brief Description: Comprehensive online safety framework establishing the eSafety Commissioner's authority. Industry codes for unlawful material (Phase 1, 2023) and age-restricted material (Phase 2, 2025) now registered and enforceable. Covers cyberbullying, image-based abuse, and illegal content.
fields.Next Steps: In force. Phase 1 unlawful material codes enforceable since December 2023. Phase 2 age-restricted material codes registered June-September 2025, enforceable from December 2025.
fields.Scope: Online service providers including social media platforms, search engines, hosting services, app distribution platforms, messaging services, and internet carriage services operating in Australia.
fields.Enforcement: eSafety Commissioner. Investigation powers for code breaches. Basic Online Safety Expectations set enforceable transparency and safety standards for platforms.
fields.Relevance Notes: Australia's comprehensive online safety framework requires industry compliance with codes and standards enforced by the eSafety Commissioner. Platforms must detect and remove illegal content including terrorist material, exploitation content, and cyber-abuse. Revontulet provides the content classification intelligence and threat analysis that supports platforms' compliance with Australian online safety obligations.
